title: salix reticulata var. normalis
intro: Salix reticulata var. normalis, commonly known as the red-vein willow or white willow, is a variety of the willow tree that is native to Europe and Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antipyretic, making it effective in treating conditions such as headaches, fever, and arthritis. The herbal preparations of Salix reticulata var. normalis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin conditions, or taken internally to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
